description:
You are an infrastructure engineer who has just cloned a totally empty repository located at
    /home/user/infra.  
    Your goal is to bootstrap a minimal local automation workflow that other engineers can reuse
    with a single   make   command.

    1. Inside /home/user/infra create a file literally named “Makefile” (there is **no file
       extension**).  
       a.  The file MUST start with the line shown below **exactly**; any difference in spelling,
           case, extra spaces, or missing characters will be considered a failure:  
           .PHONY: init deploy clean  
       b.  Immediately after that line, define three targets in the exact order
           init, deploy, clean.  
           •  Each recipe line has to be indented with **one real tab character (ASCII 0x09)** –
              **do NOT** use spaces.  
           •  There must be **no blank line** between the target header and its tab-indented recipe
              line, but there must be **one blank line** between targets.  
           •  The commands executed by each target have to be precisely the single-line recipes
              listed below.  Use   &&   exactly as shown and redirect output exactly as shown:
              init:
              (tab)@echo "Initializing infrastructure..." && touch state.tf

              deploy:
              (tab)@echo "Deployment successful" > deploy.log

              clean:
              (tab)@echo "Cleaning up..." && rm -f state.tf deploy.log
           •  Do not add any extra commands, flags, comments, or whitespace.

    2. After you finish authoring the Makefile, create a plain-text log file
       /home/user/infra/automation_setup.log whose contents are **exactly** the two lines below
       (case-sensitive, no extra whitespace, each line terminated by a single ‘\n’ newline):
       [OK] Makefile present
       [OK] init deploy clean targets configured

    3. Leave all permissions at their default values (normally 0644 for files you create).

    The automated test suite will verify BOTH files in a byte-for-byte manner, so pay careful
    attention to:
       •  Tab vs. space indentation inside the Makefile  
       •  No trailing spaces at the ends of lines  
       •  The presence of exactly one blank line between each target block  
       •  The exact text (including capitalisation and punctuation) of the
          automation_setup.log file.  

    You do not need to execute   make   itself – only author the required files.  When you have
    finished, you can print any confirmation message you like to the console; that output is not
    graded.  Your solution will be evaluated solely by examining the filesystem.
truth:
<!-- Expected path and file names -->
    <dir path="/home/user/infra" />

    <!-- Expected Makefile content with literal tab characters shown here as \t for readability -->
    <file path="/home/user/infra/Makefile"><![CDATA[
.PHONY: init deploy clean
init:
\t@echo "Initializing infrastructure..." && touch state.tf

deploy:
\t@echo "Deployment successful" > deploy.log

clean:
\t@echo "Cleaning up..." && rm -f state.tf deploy.log
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected verification log -->
    <file path="/home/user/infra/automation_setup.log"><![CDATA[
[OK] Makefile present
[OK] init deploy clean targets configured
]]></file>
